title: Add repo-aware supported app picker to the loaders
summary: Extend the static and native HumbleStudio loaders so they can offer direct selection of a few known supported Humble apps instead of requiring only manual file or URL entry.
acceptance_criteria: The web fallback loader renders a visible supported-app picker alongside the existing URL, file, and demo inputs.
The native remote URL sheets render the same curated supported-app picker and prefill the selected app URL.
Known repo-aware app entries can load their raw `.humblebundle` or `.humble/design.json` source directly.
The picker can also prefill the URL field for transparent manual inspection before loading.
Remember/reload loader behavior stays truthful for the new supported-app source type.
The static web fallback integrity check still passes after the loader changes.

evidence.note: This slice keeps the picker intentionally small and truthful by only listing neighboring repos with confirmed `.humble` exports.
Web fallback integrity validation should pass after the supported-app picker wiring.
Patch hygiene should stay clean after the loader and documentation edits.

The native macOS/iOS remote URL sheets now use the same curated app catalog as the web fallback, can either prefill or directly load a supported app, and keep truthful source labels through StudioStrings.
Repo contract validation passed with only the existing localization heuristic warning.

evidence.path: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
evidence.note: Native macOS build passed after adding StudioSupportedAppCatalog.swift to the Xcode project sources.

trigger.source_ref: User asked whether HumbleStudio could offer direct selection of supported apps instead of only file selection.

commands: xcodebuild -project HumbleStudio.xcodeproj -scheme HumbleStudioMac -configuration Debug CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
agent_notes: The current picker is intentionally curated rather than dynamic so the web fallback does not overclaim repository support.
A later native/web shared source catalog would be a good follow-up if more repos start exporting stable `.humble` sources.
The SwiftUI picker now supports both transparent URL prefilling and one-step loading so repo-aware source selection stays truthful without forcing manual URL confirmation.
